LAW AND MOTION 301, ATTY. STRAUSS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO PRESENT TO ATTY. ZACKS THAT WOULD EMBODY THE COURT'S RULING WITH RESPECT TO THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND EXPLAINING IN DETAIL THE PLAN FOR AVOIDING ANY CONSEQUENCES TO THE PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY, THE BOND, THE INSURANCE CONDITIONS AS WELL AS THE CLEAN UP CONDITION WHICH IS AN OPTION ON THE PLAINTIFF. THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER REMAINS IN EFFECT UNLESS AND UNTIL THE COURT SIGNS AN ORDER DEALING WITH THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THIS MATTER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 14, 2007. IF THE PROPOSED ORDER IS APPROVED AS TO FORM, THEN THE HEARING WILL BE VACATED. IF NOT, FURTHER HEARING ON THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WILL TAKE PLACE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Off Calendar Jan-22-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ANDREW M. ZACKS, ESQ.(ZACKS UTRECHT & LEADBETTER), (415)956-8100, FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY.
DOUGLAS C. STRAUS, ESQ.(ARCHER NORRIS), (925)930-6600, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF 1169 MARKET STREET LP'S Notice Of Application For Order Holding Defendants In Contempt Of Preliminary Injunction Orders And For Order Modifying Preliminary Injunction Orders. THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANT 1160 MISSION ASSOCIATES IN CONTEMPT FOR FAILING TO PREVENT ITS AGENTS' ENTRY ON PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. DEFENDANT IS FINE $500. ALL OTHER REQUESTED RELIEF IS DENIED. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB/LB)
